programme.title: GP Austria: Gara
programme.desc: Undicesima prova del mondiale 2025: sul circuito di proprieta' della Red Bull tanti punti di sorpasso e gare sempre spettacolari. Telecronaca di Carlo Vanzini, Marc Gene' e Roberto Chinchero.

programme.title: Austria 2016 - F1
programme.desc: 21 appuntamenti per scalare la vetta della classifica. Si accende il semaforo verde e si alzano i decibel in pista. In pole position Hamilton, in prima fila anche Rosberg.

programme.title: Bologna e i suoi colli
programme.desc: S5 Ep7 Bologna e i suoi colli - Bruno Barbieri attraversa l'Italia per valutare gli hotel del Paese esprimendo un parere su location, servizi, camere e prezzi.

programme.title: Parma: la terra dei castelli
programme.desc: S5 Ep3 Parma: La terra dei castelli - Valerio e Antonella hanno deciso di acquistare un casale nella zona dei castelli di Parma, dove pianura, piccole valli e dolci colline fanno da sfondo a rocche e antichi manieri.

programme.title: Amici miei
programme.desc: S9 Ep4 ABKS Venezia - Tantissimi ospiti d'eccezione per una serie di portate strepitose! La cucina di ABKS ospitera' chef del calibro di Stefano Cavada, Renato Bosco e Antonio Grazioli.

programme.title: Amici miei
programme.desc: S9 Ep5 Kids - Tantissimi ospiti d'eccezione per una serie di portate strepitose! La cucina di ABKS ospitera' chef del calibro di Stefano Cavada, Renato Bosco e Antonio Grazioli.

programme.title: Ep. 8
programme.desc: S23 Ep8 - Per la prima volta in assoluto, diciotto talentuosi Capi Chef professionisti, si sfideranno ad altissimi livelli per diventare il Capo Chef del ristorante Hell's Kitchen.

programme.title: Ep. 9
programme.desc: S23 Ep9 - Per la prima volta in assoluto, diciotto talentuosi Capi Chef professionisti, si sfideranno ad altissimi livelli per diventare il Capo Chef del ristorante Hell's Kitchen.

programme.title: Ep. 4
programme.desc: S1 Ep4 - Renato Bosco ospita Vincenzo La Porta, pizzaiolo siciliano che ha vinto un premio nel campionato dell'Expocook di Palermo. La sua specialita'? La pizza napoletana contemporanea!

programme.title: Ep. 5
programme.desc: S1 Ep5 - Renato Bosco ospitera' Pasquale Petrillo, pizzaiolo campano, arrivato in finale nel concorso 'Emergente chef pizza 2023'. La sua specialita'? La pizza napoletana contemporanea!

programme.title: Tra Polignano e Monopoli: le perle dell'Adriatico
programme.desc: S5 Ep4 Tra Polignano e Monopoli: Le perle dell'Adriatico - Tommaso e Monica vogliono acquistare una seconda casa in Puglia. I nostri esperti, tra trulli e masserie, cercheranno la soluzione migliore.

programme.title: Formentera: bellezza selvaggia
programme.desc: S5 Ep5 Formentera: Bellezza selvaggia - Le sorelle Brusaferro sono alla ricerca di una seconda casa nell'isola hippie di Formentera. Giulia e Nicola sono pronti a sfidarsi tra sabbie rosa e alte scogliere...

programme.title: Ep.04
programme.desc: S8 Ep4 - Le quattro giovani spose protagoniste di questo episodio promettono quattro scatenatissime feste tra amici a base di musica, balli, cocktail e tanta leggerezza.

programme.title: Ep. 12
programme.desc: S2 Ep12 - Due coppie di cuochi amatoriali dovranno trasformare per un giorno la propria casa in un vero Home Restaurant. A giudicarli Giorgio Locatelli ed Enzo Miccio.

programme.title: Bologna e i suoi colli

programme.title: Alghero
programme.desc: S7 Ep12 Alghero - Alessandro e' ad Alghero, dove l'influenza spagnola dura dal 1300. Roberta, Danilo, Andrea e Daniele si contendono il titolo di miglior ristorante di cucina sardo-catalana.

programme.title: Ep. 5
programme.desc: S15 Ep5 - Nel corso della prima sfida Mystery Box della stagione, le undici coppie migliori devono combinare degli ingredienti per creare un piatto armonioso.

programme.title: Ep. 6
programme.desc: S15 Ep6 - Le dieci coppie migliori, in una splendida cornice sul molo, hanno il compito di sfamare oltre 100 pescatori locali. La squadra con le prestazioni peggiori dovra' affrontare il temuto Pressure Test.

programme.title: Val D'Aosta

programme.title: Gubbio
programme.desc: S8 Ep2 Gubbio - Alessandro Borghese scopre la proposta culinaria di Gubbio. Riccardo, Paolo, Silvia e Gaetano difenderanno la tradizione o si spingeranno oltre le mura della cittadella?

programme.title: Milano
programme.desc: S8 Ep3 Milano - Alessandro e' alla ricerca del miglior ristorante di pesce a Milano: la citta' del mercato ittico piu' grande d'Italia! Filippo, Giulia, Nicolas e Danilo portano il mare sulla tavola.

programme.title: Fuerteventura
programme.desc: S8 Ep4 Fuerteventura - A Fuerteventura vive una comunita' nostrana di quasi 10mila persone! Alessandro, Nicoletta, Massimo e Giovanni si contendono il titolo di miglior ristorante italiano.

HotelPlanner Tour Golf Highlights
programme.desc: Highlights from the Interwetten Open during the 2026 season of the HotelPlanner Tour, the second tier of men's professional golf in Europe.

programme.title: PGA Tour Champions Highlights
programme.desc: Tournament highlights from the Principal Charity Classic - a PGA Tour Champions event held at the Wakonda Club. Spain's Miguel Angel Jiménez conquered the rest a year ago.

programme.title: US Open Golf Final Round Hlts
programme.desc: The final round of the 126th US Open Championship at Shinnecock Hills Golf Club. JJ Spaun snatched Major glory last year with a two-shot win over Scotland's Robert MacIntyre. (21.06)

programme.title: School of Golf
programme.desc: The School of Golf offers tips and tricks on how to improve your golfing game. Here, get to grips with the basics that shape the best golfers. Ep 1.

programme.title: School of Golf
programme.desc: The School of Golf offers tips and tricks on how to improve your golfing game. Here, discover some of the game's best-kept short game secrets. Ep 2.

programme.title: Golf Academy: Danny Willett
programme.desc: 2016 Masters champion Danny Willett joins the Golf Academy to discuss his career and give some helpful advice.

programme.title: Golf Academy: Faldo Iron Game
programme.desc: The Golf Academy offers guidance on how to make your golfing game even stronger. Here, Major champion Sir Nick Faldo reveals the secrets of good iron game. Ep. 2.

programme.title: Best of The Sky Zone
programme.desc: A look at all the best moments from the Sky Zone at an eventful US Open at the world-famous Pebble Beach Golf Links in California.

programme.title: Best of The Women's Openzone
programme.desc: Revisit the best of the Women's Open Zone at the 46th iteration of the tournament in East Lothian, Scotland - the scene of Ashleigh Buhai's victory at the final Major of the year.

programme.title: Playing Lessons: Corey Conners
programme.desc: Former and current pros take viewers behind the ropes on the course, discussing topics such as shot setup, club selection, and strategy. Here, Corey Conners delivers the lesson.

programme.title: Playing Lessons: Patty Tavatanakit
programme.desc: Former and current pros take viewers behind the ropes on the course, discussing such things as shot setup, club selection, and strategy. Here, Patty Tavatanakit leads the lesson.

programme.title: The Golf Fitness Show
programme.desc: An often-overlooked part of golf, fitness can improve your game in all sorts of ways. The Golf Fitness Show demonstrates the perfect exercises for the course.

programme.title: Mark Roe's Short Game School

programme.desc: Portsmouth: Enjoy the greatest Premier League game for each of the league's 47 clubs, as voted for by fans. For Portsmouth, the 4-1 win over local rivals Southampton back in April 2005.

programme.title: PL Greatest: QPR/Liverpool 2012
programme.desc: QPR: Enjoy the greatest Premier League game for each of the league's 47 clubs, as voted for by fans. For QPR, the 3-2 win over Liverpool that saw Rangers score three in the last 13 minutes.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:Reading/WHU '07
programme.desc: Reading: Enjoy the greatest Premier League game for each of the league's 47 clubs, as voted for by fans. Here, for Reading, the 6-0 hammering of West Ham at the Madejski Stadium in 2007.

programme.title: Premier League Review

programme.title: FNF: Aston Villa v Liverpool Hlts
programme.desc: Top-five rivals Aston Villa and Liverpool meet on Friday Night Football. With two Premier League games left to play, both sides are four points clear of sixth-placed Bournemouth. (15.05)

programme.title: PL: Man Utd v Nott'm Forest Hlts
programme.desc: With Champions League football to look forward to next season, Manchester United's final home game of the campaign comes against Forest - who have guaranteed Premier League safety. (17.05)

programme.title: PL: Brentford v C. Palace Hlts
programme.desc: European hopefuls Brentford welcome Conference League finalists Palace to the Gtech Community Stadium in the Premier League. The Bees come from a 3-0 loss to Manchester City. (17.05)

programme.title: PL: Everton v Sunderland Hlts
programme.desc: Everton, still fighting for a place in Europe, host Sunderland at the Hill Dickinson Stadium. The Toffees were left frustrated at Selhurst last weekend, twice relinquishing a lead. (17.05)

programme.title: PL: Leeds Utd v Brighton Hlts
programme.desc: Brighton target three more points in their pursuit of Europe as Fabian Hurzeler's men, 3-0 winners over Wolves last week, visit a Leeds side guaranteed of Premier League safety. (17.05)

programme.title: PL: Wolves v Fulham Hlts
programme.desc: Fulham go to relegated Wolves following a costly defeat to Bournemouth. With chances of Europe now slim, the Cottagers must win their last two and hope for favours elsewhere. (17.05)

programme.title: PL: Newcastle Utd v West Ham Hlts
programme.desc: With time running out for a West Ham side deep in relegation trouble, Nuno Espirito Santo's Irons visit Tyneside for a must-win Premier League encounter against Newcastle. (17.05)

programme.title: PL: Arsenal v Burnley Hlts
programme.desc: Firmly in control of the title race, Premier League leaders Arsenal entertain relegated Burnley on Monday Night Football in front of an expectant Emirates crowd. (18.05)

programme.title: PL: Bournemouth v Man City Hlts
programme.desc: With it all on the line at Bournemouth's Vitality Stadium, City arrive knowing nothing short of victory hands Arsenal the title. Can Pep Guardiola's side take it to the final day? (19.05)

programme.title: PL: Chelsea v Spurs Hlts
programme.desc: On the verge of safety, Spurs seek a point from their final two games, starting with a trip to bitter London rivals Chelsea - set for the summer arrival of new manager Xabi Alonso. (19.05)

programme.title: PL: Burnley v Wolves Hlts
programme.desc: Burnley and Wolves, both relegated and bound for the Championship, bring their respective campaigns to a close at Turf Moor. The winner here would finish up in 19th place. (24.05)

programme.title: PL: Fulham v Newcastle Hlts
programme.desc: Both out of European contention, Fulham and Newcastle meet at Craven Cottage on the final day to draw a line under disappointing 2025-26 campaigns. (24.05)

programme.title: PL: Nott'm Forest v B'mouth Hlts
programme.desc: With a place among Europe's elite a real possibility, Andoni Iraola's Bournemouth reign concludes on the road away to Forest - set for another season in the top flight. (24.05)

programme.title: PL: Sunderland v Chelsea Hlts
programme.desc: At the Stadium of Light, Sunderland and Chelsea enter a final-day showdown with both sides in the thick of the race for European football next season. (24.05)

programme.title: PL: Brighton v Man Utd Hlts
programme.desc: Targeting maximum points, Fabian Hurzeler's Brighton - desperate for final-day fixtures to go their way in the race for Europe - host Michael Carrick's third-placed United. (24.05)

programme.title: PL: Man City v Aston Villa Hlts
programme.desc: Leaving behind a remarkable legacy having won 20 major trophies in 10 years at the club, Pep Guardiola takes charge of his final City game against Europa League winners Villa. (24.05)

programme.title: PL: Spurs v Everton Hlts
programme.desc: Tottenham's fight to avoid the drop goes down to the final day. At home to impressive away performers Everton, Spurs hold a two-point advantage over relegation rivals West Ham. (24.05)

programme.title: PL: West Ham Utd v Leeds Utd Hlts
programme.desc: On the brink of a disastrous relegation from the Premier League, West Ham must win here against Leeds and hope relegation rivals Spurs lose at home to Everton. (24.05)

programme.title: PL: C. Palace v Arsenal Hlts
programme.desc: Arsenal's crowning moment comes at Selhurst Park, where the Premier League winners take on a Crystal Palace side looking ahead to next week's Conference League final. (24.05)

programme.title: PL: Liverpool v Brentford Hlts
programme.desc: A frustrating campaign for Arne Slot and Liverpool concludes at home to European hopefuls Brentford. Almost home and dry, the Reds are still sweating on their Champions League spot. (24.05)

programme.desc: The incredible story of Brighton & Hove Albion; from the brink of collapse and years in the lower leagues to becoming one of the Premier League's smartest and most admired clubs.

programme.title: FNF: Leeds v Burnley Hlts
programme.desc: Hitting the 40-point mark last week, Daniel Farke's Leeds bid to take another step towards Premier League safety on Friday Night Football against already-relegated Burnley. (01.05)

programme.title: PL: Brentford v West Ham Hlts
programme.desc: Fighting the drop, West Ham head to London rivals Brentford. The Irons remain two points above the Premier League relegation zone, despite a massive win over Everton last weekend. (02.05)

programme.title: PL: Bournemouth v C. Palace Hlts
programme.desc: Andoni Iraola's European hopefuls Bournemouth host Oliver Glasner's Crystal Palace. The Cherries were denied a third consecutive win late on by Leeds last week. (03.05)

programme.title: PL: Wolves v Sunderland Hlts
programme.desc: Unai Emery's Aston Villa visit relegated Burnley on Super Sunday looking to avoid a third consecutive Premier League defeat in their pursuit of Champions League qualification. (10.05)

programme.title: PL: Newcastle v Brighton Hlts
programme.desc: Denied victory at the last on Monday, Everton - winless in four - venture to Selhurst Park on Super Sunday for a clash with Oliver Glasner's Crystal Palace in the Premier League. (10.05)

programme.title: SNF: Arsenal v Fulham Hlts
programme.desc: Back on top, Arsenal look to open up a six-point lead over City - who play Monday - with the Saturday Night Football visit of Fulham, in a fight of their own for a place in Europe. (02.05)

programme.title: PL: Man Utd v Liverpool Hlts
programme.desc: A fierce rivalry renewed with Champions League qualification the aim for both, Manchester United and Liverpool lock horns again on a huge Super Sunday at Old Trafford. (03.05)

programme.title: PL: Chelsea v Nott'm Forest Hlts
programme.desc: FA Cup finalists Chelsea, under caretaker charge of Calum McFarlane, return to Premier League action against Vitor Pereira's Forest, hitting their stride at just the right time. (04.05)

programme.title: MNF: Everton v Man City Hlts
programme.desc: Manchester City's title pursuit continues at the Hill Dickinson Stadium on Monday Night Football against Everton. Guardiola's City still hold a game in hand over leaders Arsenal. (04.05)

programme.title: PL: Brighton v Wolves Hlts
programme.desc: A chance for Brighton to return to winning ways after seeing their remarkable run of form ended at St James' Park as Fabian Hurzeler's Seagulls welcome relegated Wolves. (09.05)

programme.title: PL: Fulham v Bournemouth Hlts
programme.desc: Fulham and Bournemouth - separated by four points - meet at the Cottage. The Cherries stretched their club record unbeaten run to 15 games last week, winning 3-0 against Palace. (09.05)

programme.title: PL: Sunderland v Man Utd Hlts
programme.desc: With Champions League football guaranteed next season, Michael Carrick's Manchester United go to Sunderland, whose hopes of Europe took a hit with a draw away to Wolves last week. (09.05)

programme.title: PL: Burnley v Aston Villa Hlts
programme.desc: Unai Emery's Aston Villa visit relegated Burnley on Super Sunday looking to avoid a third consecutive Premier League defeat in their pursuit of Champions League qualification. (10.05)

programme.title: PL: Crystal Palace v Everton Hlts
programme.desc: Denied victory at the last on Monday, Everton - winless in four - venture to Selhurst Park on Super Sunday for a clash with Oliver Glasner's Crystal Palace in the Premier League. (10.05)

programme.title: SNF: Man City v Brentford Hlts
programme.desc: Dropping points on the road earlier this week, it's another must-win contest for Pep Guardiola's title hopefuls City as Brentford arrive at the Etihad on Saturday Night Football. (09.05)

programme.title: PL: Nott'm Forest v Newcastle Hlts
programme.desc: On Super Sunday at the City Ground, Vitor Pereira's in-form Forest look to take another major step towards survival with the Premier League visit of Eddie Howe's Newcastle. (10.05)

programme.title: PL: West Ham v Arsenal Hlts
programme.desc: Arsenal's pursuit of the double takes them to the London Stadium on Super Sunday as Mikel Arteta's Premier League leaders face a West Ham side fighting for top-flight survival. (10.05)

programme.title: MNF: Spurs v Leeds Hlts
programme.desc: Following another pressure-relieving victory for Spurs, it's Leeds next at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium as the hosts target a third consecutive win in their fight for survival. (11.05)
